content.text:
I'll start by exploring the codebase to understand its structure and warm up for the planning task.

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Glob">
<parameter name="pattern">**/*.{ts,tsx}</parameter>
<parameter name="limit">50</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>
<thinking>
The user wants me to warm up by exploring the codebase. Let me start by looking at the project structure.
</thinking>

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Read">
<parameter name="file_path">/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/README.md</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Bash">
<parameter name="command">ls -la /Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Bash">
<parameter name="command">ls -la /Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/client/src/</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>

I've explored the basic structure. The project is a TypeScript monorepo with:
- **client/** - React frontend application
- **server/** - Node.js Express backend with Prisma ORM
- Uses yarn as package manager
- PostgreSQL database with Redis for queues/caching

The client uses Vite for building, React Router for navigation, and has a component-based architecture with pages, features, and shared components.

I'm ready to help with any planning or architecture tasks. What would you like me to explore or design?
